intro: Official licensing procedures in Argentina involve variable costs across jurisdictions, processing times, and required documentation. Understanding these details upfront saves you unnecessary trips and surprises. Getting or renewing a driver's license has costs that vary by category and province. Our 2026 rates are current and regularly reviewed to keep you informed.

explanation:
Official licensing procedures in Argentina are regulated by different agencies (RENAPER, DNRPA, AFIP, Migrations, Civil Registry, etc.), each with their own costs, timelines, and documentation requirements. This calculator provides an estimate of the total cost and necessary documents for your specific license type and category.

## How It's Calculated

This calculator applies the standard formula using your selected values, verifying typical ranges and alerting you to any values that seem unusual or outside normal parameters.

## Important Notes

This calculation is for reference only. For critical decisions affecting your finances, health, or legal status, consult with a qualified professional. Values are updated for 2026 and reviewed regularly to maintain accuracy.

faq.q: Do I need a medical exam for a driver's license?
faq.a: Yes, a psychophysical exam is mandatory, including vision, hearing, blood pressure, and reflexes testing.

faq.q: How long is a driver's license valid?
faq.a: Five years for first-time licenses. Renewal validity is 2-5 years depending on age, with shorter periods for seniors.

faq.q: Is a driver's theory course required?
faq.a: Yes, a theory course is mandatory for new licenses in most provinces.

faq.q: What is the points or demerit scoring system?
faq.a: Licenses use a point system where infractions result in point deductions, which can impact your renewal eligibility.

faq.q: Do professional categories require additional testing?
faq.a: Yes, professional categories (C, D, E for commercial transport) require more rigorous medical exams.

faq.q: Do I need an appointment to apply for a license?
faq.a: Yes, most agencies require advance appointments booked online through argentina.gob.ar or the specific agency's website. Walk-ins are accepted only for emergencies.

faq.q: How long does processing typically take?
faq.a: Processing varies by application type: standard applications take 2-4 weeks, while express processing is available for an additional fee.

faq.q: Can I apply for a license online?
faq.a: Some services are available online (pre-registration, document requests) but most applications require an in-person visit for biometric capture and exam administration.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology
